response.justification: Strong candidate with direct AI-native infrastructure experience currently at re:cinq, demonstrating the exact baseline competency the role requires (agentic systems and frameworks). Proven distributed systems expertise from Dunnhumby's high-throughput microservices environment and strong ownership mindset across 12+ years of full-stack work. Critical gap: Python (must-have) not mentioned on resume despite strong TypeScript; operational ownership and on-call experience also not explicitly documented. These should be clarified in interview, but current role fit and technical foundation support moving forward.
strengths: Currently building AI-agent infrastructure and MCP frameworks at re:cinq—directly aligned with the role's focus on agentic code production and technical strategy

Demonstrated expertise in distributed systems and high-throughput architectures from Dunnhumby role handling millions of HTTP requests per second with microservices, async I/O, caching, and real-time databases
Strong ownership mindset with 12+ years of full-stack engineering, TDD/CI/CD practices, and track record of founding and maintaining open-source projects
concerns: Python is not mentioned on the resume despite being a must-have 'strong Python' requirement; only TypeScript explicitly listed as current primary language
No explicit evidence of operational ownership, monitoring practices, or out-of-hours on-call rotation experience—a required non-technical skill
Recently joined re:cinq in April 2025 and still in early tenure with AI-native environments, though extensive background mitigates this
